sources: BallDontLie PGA API (GOAT tier — players, field, odds, props, stats)

players.name: Xander Schauffele
players.notes: Most well-rounded player on Tour. No weakness. Excels at Torrey Pines.

players.name: Collin Morikawa
players.notes: Second-best iron player. Low ball flight suits firm/windy. Premium bogey under play.

players.name: Rory McIlroy
players.notes: Best driver on Tour. High birdie ceiling but volatile. Great for matchups.

players.name: Patrick Cantlay
players.notes: Elite putter. Lowest bogey rate. Best bogey under play on Tour. Riviera specialist.

players.name: Ludvig Aberg
players.notes: Young star with elite power. Short game still developing. High upside.

players.name: Viktor Hovland
players.notes: Great ball-striker but short game liability. Negative SG:ARG. Risky bogey unders.

players.name: Tommy Fleetwood
players.notes: Links-style player. Low ball flight suits windy conditions. Good bogey under candidate.

players.name: Sahith Theegala
players.notes: High birdie upside but high bogey rate. Very aggressive. Best for birdie overs.

players.name: Adam Scott
players.notes: Veteran with elite swing. Augusta specialist. Still competitive but ceiling has lowered.

players.name: Sam Burns
players.notes: Talented ball-striker with streaky putting. When putter is hot, can contend anywhere.

players.name: Justin Thomas
players.notes: Former world #1 in a slump. Ball-striking still elite but putter has gone cold. High ceiling, low floor.

players.name: Sepp Straka
players.notes: Steady ball-striker from Austria. No standout category. Reliable for MC and top-20 at mid-strength events.

players.name: Keegan Bradley
players.notes: Steady veteran. Consistent without being flashy. Good MC and top-20 candidate.

players.name: Cameron Young
players.notes: Huge power but accuracy issues. Multiple runner-up finishes. Volatile scorer — birdie overs at long courses.

players.name: Hideki Matsuyama
players.notes: Masters champion. Elite approach play with high ball flight. Streaky putter — hot weeks can win anywhere.

players.name: Jason Day
players.notes: Resurgent veteran. Short game wizard when healthy. Bay Hill specialist. Good at TPC Sawgrass.

players.name: Max Homa
players.notes: Solid all-around. Riviera and Torrey specialist. Reliable at home courses.

players.name: Sungjae Im
players.notes: Iron man — plays every week. Rarely misses cuts. Great for MC and top 20 props.

players.name: Corey Conners
players.notes: Elite iron player but can't putt. Highest GIR with lowest conversion. Great approach stats, fade putting props.

players.name: Matt Fitzpatrick
players.notes: US Open champion. Precision player. Not long but very accurate. Thrives on tight, demanding courses.

players.name: Brian Harman
players.notes: Open champion. Lefty with great short game. Not a power player. Thrives on accuracy courses and links.

players.name: Tony Finau
players.notes: Elite power but inconsistent approach and flat-stick. Best at bombers courses. Fade candidate at short tracks.

players.name: Akshay Bhatia
players.notes: Young lefty talent. Aggressive. Putting holds him back. High birdie ceiling with high floor.

players.name: Si Woo Kim
players.notes: Versatile Korean player with multiple wins. Solid all-around game. Steady at Augusta but ceiling is top-10. Reliable MC prop.

players.name: Denny McCarthy
players.notes: Best putter on Tour. Short off the tee. Needs accuracy courses where length doesn't matter. Elite bogey under.

players.name: Lucas Glover
players.notes: US Open champion (2009). Augusta veteran who plays with grinder mentality. Consistent MC maker. T8 here proves he can compete.

players.name: Min Woo Lee
players.notes: Australian talent with power. Sister Minjee is LPGA star. Inconsistent but can go low. Good for T20 at mid-tier.

players.name: Robert MacIntyre
players.notes: Scottish lefty with links pedigree. Gritty competitor. Good in wind. Putter can go cold.

players.name: Harris English
players.notes: Steady veteran. Good all-around game without elite skill. East Lake familiarity. Reliable for MC at full fields.

players.name: Ben Griffin
players.notes: Steady mid-tier player. No outstanding skill. MC candidate at weaker fields. Fade at marquee events.

players.name: Taylor Pendrith
players.notes: Bomber who relies on length. Weak short game. Best at wide-open bombers courses. Fade at precision tracks.

players.name: Alex Noren
players.notes: Auto-added from tournament field.

players.name: Michael Kim
players.notes: Auto-added from tournament field.

players.name: Jordan Spieth
players.notes: 3x major champ in a slump. Course history still matters — elite at Augusta, Colonial, Pebble. Buy low candidate.

players.name: J.J. Spaun
players.notes: Auto-added from tournament field.

players.name: Ryan Fox
players.notes: New Zealand bomber. Son of Grant Fox. Huge off the tee but Augusta short game demands more. Better on longer bombers tracks.

players.name: Daniel Berger
players.notes: Auto-added from tournament field.

players.name: Kurt Kitayama
players.notes: Auto-added from tournament field.

players.name: Sam Stevens
players.notes: Auto-added from tournament field.

players.name: Mackenzie Hughes
players.notes: Canadian precision player. Limited Augusta experience. Consistent MC candidate. No contender ceiling yet.

players.name: Nicolai Højgaard
players.notes: Auto-added from tournament field.

players.name: Ryan Gerard
players.notes: Auto-added from tournament field.

players.name: Matt Wallace
players.notes: Auto-added from tournament field.

players.name: Chris Gotterup
players.notes: Auto-added from tournament field.

players.name: Pierceson Coody
players.notes: Auto-added from tournament field.

players.name: Jake Knapp
players.notes: Longest hitter on Tour. Accuracy is a problem. Short game is Tour-worst tier. Only play at bomber-friendly tracks.

players.name: Maverick McNealy
players.notes: Stanford product. West Coast familiarity helps. Pebble and Torrey specialist. Thin field MC candidate.

players.name: J.T. Poston
players.notes: Ball-striking specialist. Consistent mid-field at Augusta. No major breakthrough yet. Solid MC prop.

players.name: Jacob Bridgeman
players.notes: Former Clemson standout. Athletic young player building tour experience. High ceiling but still raw.

players.name: Matt McCarty
players.notes: Auto-added from tournament field.

news.headline: Betting the Truist Championship: Best bets, tips and more
news.description: All of the traits bettors should be looking for at the Truist Championship, and the best bets to make.

news.headline: Bryson DeChambeau plans to focus on YouTube if LIV Golf ends
news.description: Two-time major champion Bryson DeChambeau said if LIV Golf dissolves he plans to focus on growing his YouTube channel.

news.headline: Catch the PGA Championship on ESPN
news.description: Watch the PGA Championship on ESPN and the ESPN App from May 14-17.

news.headline: 2026 Truist Championship: How to watch, PGA schedule
news.description: The Truist Championship, the sixth signature event of the PGA Tour season, gets underway this week at the Quail Hollow Club in Charlotte, North Carolina.

news.headline: Truist Championship preview
news.description: Take a look at the important news and notes ahead of the Truist Championship.

news.headline: Phil Mickelson out, Max Homa in for next week's PGA Championship
news.description: Phil Mickelson, a six-time major champion, has withdrawn from next week's PGA Championship, paving the way for Max Homa's entry into the tournament.

news.headline: Jon Rahm eligible for Ryder Cup after settling DP World Tour dispute
news.description: Jon Rahm revealed that he has resolved his dispute with the DP World Tour, a development that will allow the two-time major champion to compete for the European team in next year's Ryder Cup.

news.headline: Jon Rahm on LIV Golf contract: 'I don't see many ways out'
news.description: Jon Rahm said he has multiple years left on his contract with LIV Golf and doesn't see "many ways out," as the league attempts to find new financing.

news.headline: PGA Tour's 2027 winter schedule looking a lot like this year's
news.description: The PGA Tour's 2027 winter schedule is looking similar to this year's slate, minus a previous stop in Hawaii and some questions remaining over Florida's place in the early rotation.

news.headline: Stewart Cink wins PGA Tour Champions' Regions Tradition
news.description: Stewart Cink closed with a 3-under 69 for a three-shot victory in the Regions Tradition for his second major this year on the PGA Tour Champions.

news.headline: Cameron Young finishes wire-to-wire win at Cadillac Championship
news.description: Cameron Young went wire-to-wire alone on the lead at the Cadillac Championship, a final round of 4-under 68 getting him to 19 under for the week and six shots clear of world No. 1 Scottie Scheffler.

news.headline: Stewart Cink builds 3-shot lead, eyes bid to win 2nd straight senior major
news.description: Stewart Cink increased his lead to three strokes Saturday in tricky wind conditions at the Regions Tradition in his bid to win consecutive senior major titles.

news.headline: Cameron Young takes 5-shot Cadillac lead into weekend at Doral
news.description: Cameron Young shot a 5-under 67 on Friday to get to 13 under and take a five-shot lead at the midway point of the Cadillac Championship.

news.headline: Stewart Cink opens up 2-shot lead in Regions Traditions
news.description: Stewart Cink had eight birdies and a bogey for a second straight 7-under 65 on Friday, opening up a two-shot lead in the Regions Tradition.

news.headline: Stewart Cink, Alex Cejka atop Champions' Regions Tradition
news.description: Stewart Cink birdied the par-5 18th for a 7-under 65 and share of the first-round lead with Alex Cejka on Thursday in the Regions Tradition, the second major of the year on the PGA Tour Champions.
